name: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: NIST SP 800-53 Revision 5 (with Update 1, December 2024) is the federal catalog of security and privacy controls for US federal information systems and organizations. The catalog contains 1,189 control entries (322 base controls plus 867 control enhancements) organized into 20 families: Access Control (AC), Awareness and Training (AT), Audit and Accountability (AU), Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring (CA), Configuration Management (CM), Contingency Planning (CP), Identification and Authentication (IA), Incident Response (IR), Maintenance (MA), Media Protection (MP), Physical and Environmental Protection (PE), Planning (PL), Program Management (PM), Personnel Security (PS), Personally Identifiable Information Processing and Transparency (PT), Risk Assessment (RA), System and Services Acquisition (SA), System and Communications Protection (SC), System and Information Integrity (SI), and Supply Chain Risk Management (SR). Each entry has a control statement, supplementary discussion, and a list of related controls. The PT and SR families were new in Rev 5.
relationToCloudSecurity: SP 800-53 r5 is the foundation of FedRAMP (which selects a subset for cloud service provider baselines) and DoD CC SRG. Cloud providers serving US federal customers map their controls to 800-53. The catalog also serves as a reference for non-federal cloud security programs — many private-sector compliance frameworks (CCM, ISO 27001 cross-walks, etc.) include 800-53 mappings.
relationToAISecurity: SP 800-53 r5 does not include AI-specific controls per se, but its scope (information systems and organizations) covers AI systems as a special case of information systems. NIST AI 600-1 (Generative AI Profile of the AI RMF) provides AI-specific risk treatment that complements 800-53 implementations.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the public domain in the United States per Section 105 of the US Copyright Act.

lifecycle.notes: Rev 5 was released September 2020 with Update 1 in December 2024. Update 1 included errata corrections without changing control IDs. Rev 4 was retired with Rev 5's release.

current_extraction.notes: Extracted from the NIST-published XLSX (sp800-53r5-controls.xlsx) which NIST distributes as a supplemental material alongside the SP 800-53r5 PDF. The XLSX is the authoritative structured form — derived from the same OSCAL source that produces NIST's machine-readable representations. Each row is either a base control (e.g., AC-1) or a control enhancement (e.g., AC-2(1)); the parser derives family, base_control, and enhancement_number fields. The companion 800-53-r5.md is a marker-extracted markdown from the PDF (retained for narrative reference but not used for structured extraction).

extraction_history.reason: Initial structured extraction from NIST-published XLSX (replaces prior markdown-only state)

notes: Critical resource — referenced by FedRAMP, DoD CC SRG, and many cross-framework mappings (CCM, CSF, ISO 27001, etc.). Use the structured CSV/JSON for mapping work; the markdown extraction is retained for narrative reference.
